Search Results for

    Show / Hide Table of Contents

    Chaining Filters

    You can chain different filter assets in one filter declaration.

    .highlighted {
    filter: filter("/Packages/com.nkstudio.uitk-effects/Scripts/Runtime/Filters/OutlineFilter.asset" 4 rgb(255, 210, 0) 0)
            filter("/Packages/com.nkstudio.uitk-effects/Scripts/Runtime/Filters/DropShadowFilter.asset" 8 8 10 rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.5));
}

    Chaining the same effect type

    When using the same FilterFunctionDefinition more than once in a single chain, use the duplicate B asset for the second entry.

    /* Avoid using the same asset twice in the same chain. */
filter: filter("...DropShadowFilter.asset" 20 20 70 rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.3))
        filter("...DropShadowFilter.asset" -20 -20 70 rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.7));

/* Use the base asset and the B variant. */
filter: filter("...DropShadowFilter.asset" 20 20 70 rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.3))
        filter("...DropShadowFilterB.asset" -20 -20 70 rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.7));

    The package includes DropShadowFilterB.asset and InnerShadowFilterB.asset for this pattern.

    In this article
